_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, comprising coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice, is currently indexed at 132 relative to 2015, indicating a 32% rise over this period. This index represents an average of price changes across these key commodities commonly consumed at breakfast. The increase has been recent and uneven across components rather than steady or uniform. Such movement highlights fluctuations specific to individual commodities rather than a broad, consistent increase.
notes.movers: Coffee and orange juice are the primary contributors to the basket's rise, with coffee prices up 93% and orange juice up 22% since 2015. Wheat and sugar have had more modest increases of 7% and 5% respectively. These price changes are largely driven by weather and crop disease impacts such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice, and frost or drought in arabica coffee-growing regions. The soft commodity markets involved are relatively thin, meaning smaller supply disruptions can cause significant price changes.
notes.why: The recent rise in the breakfast basket index is mainly attributable to a few weather-sensitive crops rather than systemic inflation across all commodities. Because these commodities respond strongly to environmental factors, price inputs can remain elevated for years if adverse conditions persist or recur. This sensitivity underscores the volatility and supply risks inherent in soft commodity markets.
